texto: Disquite SonnetI wish that I could find the words to tellYou were it hurts; nothing breaking my skinSlices whispering in my brain like hellLeaking suggestions of a morose grinCannot collect my thoughts long enough toShare them in an understandable waySo I lock my lips firmly and walk throughLife, searching for the perfect words to sayTrapped in my head, I seek to be let outGrasping connections with those who might knowWhat it feels like, alone in a crowd, doubtFilling my body with reasons to goFace to face, I might not find the right phraseBut I hope someone hears me anyway
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kathy Trithardt: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Disquite SonnetI wish that I could find the words to tellYou were it hurts; nothing breaking my s…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que inteligencia, anxiety, sonnet pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e sonnet.
